title: Tender Molten Slovakian Pork Stew
description: A hearty and flavorful Slovakian dish featuring tender pork, Italian seasoning, and spicy ferment-kimchi, perfect for satisfying midnight cravings. This ayurvedic-rajasic-friendly recipe is designed to nourish both body and soul.
instructions: Heat a large hot-pot vessel over medium-high heat and add the pork cubes, browning them on all sides.
Add the chopped onion to the pot and cook until it is translucent and starting to caramelize.
Add the minced garlic to the pot and cook for an additional minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Stir in the Italian seasoning, caraway seeds, paprika, salt, and black pepper, and cook for 1 minute.
Add the chopped ferment-kimchi to the pot and stir to combine.
Pour in the chicken broth and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer the stew, covered, for 2 hours, or until the pork is tender and the sauce has thickened.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with additional ferment-kimchi and crusty bread on the side.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
Cook pork to an internal temperature of 63°C / 145°F and let it rest for 3 minutes before serving.
